title: tradescantia axillaris var. flexuosa
intro: Tradescantia axillaris var. flexuosa, also known as the Wandering Jew or Inch Plant, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Commelinaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including soothing burns, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Tradescantia axillaris var. flexuosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in herbal remedies and traditional medicine.
